title: Cognitive Electrophysiology in Socioeconomic Context in Adulthood
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalogram (EEG) recordings from 127 young adults (18-30 years) collected during standardized cognitive tasks designed to assess perception, cognition, and action. Participants completed ERP CORE tasks or adaptations thereof, with concurrent collection of retrospective and current socioeconomic status indicators, including childhood family SES, educational attainment, income, food security, and neighborhood characteristics. The dataset also includes ADHD symptom screening data, enabling investigation of relationships between socioeconomic context, neural electrophysiology, and neurodevelopmental symptomatology in a socioeconomically diverse sample.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded from 127 young adults (18-30 years) during cognitive tasks directly acquired from or adapted from the Event-Related Potentials Compendium of Open Resources and Experiments (ERP CORE). Tasks were optimized to capture neural activity related to perception, cognition, and action. Each participant completed each EEG task once. Identifiable information including date of birth, race/ethnicity, zip code, and language information were removed prior to public release; age in years was computed and retained.

funding_references.funder_name: research start-up funds awarded to Elif Isbell by University of California Merced.
